_comment: NEC Admissions + Placements FAQ - Voice Agent Script

entries.question: What is your name?
entries.answer: I’m your voice assistant. How can I help today?

entries.question: What can you do?
entries.answer: I can answer questions, help with basic troubleshooting, and guide you through common tasks.

entries.question: How do I contact support?
entries.answer: You can contact support at support@example.com.

entries.question: What is the eligibility for B.Tech admission?
entries.answer: You must have passed 10+2 with a minimum of 45% aggregate marks, with Physics and Mathematics as mandatory subjects.

entries.question: What is the eligibility for M.Tech admission?
entries.answer: You must hold a B.E. or B.Tech degree in the relevant discipline from a recognized university with a minimum of 50% marks.

entries.question: What is the eligibility for MBA admission?
entries.answer: Any bachelor's degree with a minimum of 50% marks is required.

entries.question: What is the eligibility for MCA admission?
entries.answer: A bachelor's degree with minimum 50% marks is required, plus Mathematics as a subject in 10+2.

entries.question: How are B.Tech seats allotted?
aliases: How do I get a seat in B.Tech?
entries.answer: 70% of seats are filled through AP EAPCET ranks via online counselling. The remaining 30% are filled under B-Category management quota based on 10+2 marks.

entries.question: How are M.Tech seats allotted?
entries.answer: 70% of seats are filled through GATE or PGECET ranks via online counselling. The remaining 30% are under B-Category based on B.Tech marks.

entries.question: How are MBA and MCA seats allotted?
aliases: How are MBA MCA admissions done?
entries.answer: 70% of seats are filled through APICET ranks via online counselling. The remaining 30% are under B-Category based on qualifying exam marks.

entries.question: What is B-Category admission?
entries.answer: B-Category is the management quota. 30% of seats in every course are filled directly by the college based on merit in the qualifying exam, as per APSCHE guidelines.

entries.question: How do I apply for B-Category admission?
entries.answer: You can apply online at admissions.nrtec.in or call 915-468-6203 or email admissions@nrtec.in.

entries.question: What happens to vacant seats after counselling?
aliases: Are there seats available after counselling?
entries.answer: Any seats left vacant after the final round of convener quota counselling are filled by the college through spot admissions.

entries.question: How do I contact the admissions office?
entries.answer: Phone: 915-468-6203 or 810-630-6313. Email: admissions@nrtec.in. Website: admissions.nrtec.in.

entries.question: What B.Tech courses are available and how many seats?
entries.answer: CSE has 720 seats, CSE AI has 300, CSE AI and ML has 240, ECE has 360, IT has 120, CSE Data Science has 120, Civil has 60, EEE has 60, Mechanical has 60, and CSE Cyber Security has 60 seats.

entries.question: What PG courses are available and how many seats?
entries.answer: MCA has 180 seats, MBA has 180 seats, M.Tech CSE has 36 seats. M.Tech specializations in Structural Engineering, Thermal Engineering, Machine Design, VLSI, Power and Industrial Drives, and Digital Electronics have seats ranging from 9 to 24.

entries.question: How many seats are under convener quota and management quota for B.Tech?
entries.answer: For every B.Tech course, 70% of seats are convener quota filled via AP EAPCET counselling and 30% are management quota. For example, CSE has 504 convener seats and 216 management seats.

entries.question: Is there an NRI quota for admissions?
entries.answer: Yes. NEC offers NRI quota admissions for B.Tech. It is merit-based and follows APSCHE guidelines. Contact the admissions office at 915-468-6203 or admissions@nrtec.in for the schedule and application form.

entries.question: What entrance exam do I need for B.Tech convener quota?
aliases: Do I need EAPCET for BTech?
entries.answer: You need a valid AP EAPCET rank. Seats are allotted through online counselling conducted by APSCHE.

entries.question: What entrance exam do I need for M.Tech convener quota?
aliases: Do I need GATE for MTech?
entries.answer: You need a valid GATE or PGECET score. Seats are allotted through APSCHE online counselling.

entries.question: What entrance exam do I need for MBA or MCA convener quota?
aliases: Which exam for MBA MCA admission?
entries.answer: You need a valid APICET rank. Seats are allotted through APSCHE online counselling.

entries.question: Can I apply for admission without an entrance exam rank?
entries.answer: Yes. You can apply under B-Category management quota which is based on merit in your qualifying exam, without needing an entrance exam rank. Apply at admissions.nrtec.in.

entries.question: Is NEC approved by AICTE?
entries.answer: Yes. NEC is approved by AICTE, permanently affiliated to JNTUK Kakinada, has autonomous status, and is accredited by NAAC with an A+ grade.

entries.question: Where do I apply for admission to NEC?
entries.answer: Apply online at admissions.nrtec.in, or call 915-468-6203 or 810-630-6313, or email admissions@nrtec.in.

entries.question: Does NEC provide placement assistance?
entries.answer: Yes. NEC provides 100% placement assistance through its dedicated Training and Placement Cell, which connects students with top companies across IT and core engineering sectors.

entries.question: What companies recruit from NEC?
entries.answer: Top recruiters include Accenture, TCS, Tech Mahindra, Cognizant, Savantis Solutions, COAPPS, Rinex Technologies, ITC Ltd, and Thermopads among others.

entries.question: What is the average placement package at NEC?
aliases: What is the salary package at NEC?
How much do NEC students earn?
entries.answer: Packages vary by company. Accenture offers 4.6 to 6.5 LPA, TCS offers 3.5 to 7 LPA, Rinex Technologies offers 5.2 LPA, COAPPS offers 4 LPA, and Tech Mahindra offers 3.25 LPA.

entries.question: How many students get placed at NEC?
entries.answer: NEC has strong placement records. For example, Accenture selected 108 students, Savantis Solutions selected 60, COAPPS selected 57, TVS Brakes selected 52, and Rinex Technologies selected 23 students in recent drives.

entries.question: Does NEC provide training before placements?
aliases: How does NEC prepare students for jobs?
entries.answer: Yes. NEC's Training and Placement Cell conducts soft skills training, employability skills programs, certification programs, APSSDC training, and capability enhancement programs to prepare students for placements.

entries.question: Does NEC have an incubation center?
entries.answer: Yes. NEC has an Incubation Centre and Venture Development Centre in association with i2E North Eastern University, USA, and an entrepreneurship cell backed by TiE Hyderabad.

entries.question: Does NEC offer internship opportunities?
aliases: Can I get an internship through NEC?
entries.answer: Yes. The Training and Placement Cell actively organizes on-campus, off-campus drives, and internship opportunities for students across disciplines.

entries.question: How do I contact the placement cell at NEC?
entries.answer: You can contact the Placement Head Mr. Syam Kumar Reddy at placements@nrtec.in or call 9885553622.

entries.question: Who heads the placement cell at NEC?
entries.answer: The Placement Cell is headed by Mr. Syam Kumar Reddy Kolli, and the Training Head is Dr. Ananda Oliveti, a strategic HR and Learning and Development professional with 15 years of experience.

entries.question: Does NEC have placements in core engineering sectors?
entries.answer: Yes. NEC facilitates placements in both IT and core engineering sectors. Companies like TVS Brakes India recruit core engineering students directly from the campus.

entries.question: Does NEC have tie-ups with any industries?
entries.answer: Yes. NEC has collaborations including an Indo-European Skilling Centre with APSSDC and ARC Germany, a Venture Development Centre with i2E North Eastern University USA, and an entrepreneurship cell with TiE Hyderabad.
